claim: Cross-check confirms explorer claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements boundary audit that detects project file mutations before and after full-tool Claude agent execution and blocks PRD readiness when violations occur or evidence lacks required scope declarations. All 16 cited evidence records verified at exact line numbers.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot walks project files using gitFiles + walkFiles, computes SHA-256 digests via fileDigest, excludes allowed roots via isWithin check, and returns digest map. diffBoundarySnapshots compares before/after maps to detect added, modified, or deleted files.
evidence.why: Independent verification confirms boundary detection implementation matches explorer description exactly.

evidence.summary: Runtime generates BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers from boundaryChanges array and spreads them into finalReadiness.blockers, proving boundary audit actively blocks PRD readiness on mutations.
evidence.why: Explorer cited 516-572; cross-check found actual blocker generation at 585-594, confirming enforcement mechanism.

evidence.summary: execution_policy object declares project_mutation: 'forbidden' and allowed_write_roots restricted to output_dir only, documenting the boundary contract.
evidence.why: Confirmed policy documentation matches explorer claim exactly.

evidence.summary: Agent prompt hard rules include 'Boundary: do not modify target project files' and 'Every evidence record must include scope: project, external, user, or unknown', enforcing read-only behavior and evidence provenance.
evidence.why: Confirmed agent instructions prevent mutations and mandate scope declarations.

evidence.summary: VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES set contains exactly four values: project, external, user, unknown.
evidence.why: Confirmed scope restriction prevents unvalidated evidence types.

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordScope classifies evidence by checking scope field first, then URL patterns, then source type field. Returns project only for project_code, project_test, project_docs, project_config, project_log, project_artifact sources or repo-relative paths.
evidence.why: Confirmed classification enforces evidence requirements for existing project claims.

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues returns PROJECT_EVIDENCE_PATH_REQUIRED when scope is project but path missing, or PROJECT_FACT_REQUIRES_PROJECT_EVIDENCE when only non-project scopes present.
evidence.why: Confirmed enforcement prevents assumptions from becoming facts.

evidence.summary: evidenceScopeDeclarationIssues filters records for missing scope field via evidenceRecordHasDeclaredScope and generates EVIDENCE_SCOPE_REQUIRED blockers.
evidence.why: Confirmed validation prevents ambiguous evidence sources.

evidence.summary: requiredEvidenceRoles returns ['explorer', 'cross-checker', 'verifier'] for cross_check policy and ['explorer', 'verifier'] for single_agent.
evidence.why: Confirmed cross-check protocol forces independent verification.

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_AGENT_PROTOCOLS defines explorer, cross_checker, and verifier objects with writes_code: false and result_schema: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_RESULT_SCHEMA.
evidence.why: Confirmed protocol contract requires evidence agents to be read-only.

evidence.summary: buildProviderInvocation constructs claude, codex, or custom provider commands with tool profile args including --tools, --allowedTools, --disallowedTools for evidence agent execution.
evidence.why: Confirmed provider abstraction enables configurable tool spawning.

evidence.summary: Test 'boundary blocks project file mutation even when agent results pass' spawns agent that writes unexpected-project-write.txt, asserts result.status === 'blocked' and BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blocker present.
evidence.why: Confirmed test evidence proves boundary audit enforcement actually blocks PRD readiness.

evidence.summary: Test 'git-backed boundary detects untracked project mutations' inits git repo, spawns agent writing untracked-mutation.txt, asserts violated status and change detection.
evidence.why: Confirmed test evidence proves boundary works with git repos and untracked files.

evidence.summary: Test 'non-git boundary fallback detects modified project files and ignores artifacts' creates existing.txt, spawns agent modifying it and writing allowed artifact, asserts violated status and change detection excludes artifact.
evidence.why: Confirmed test evidence proves boundary works without git and respects allowed roots.

evidence.summary: Test 'external-only evidence cannot satisfy existing project facts' creates demand with external evidence only, asserts prd_ready === false and PROJECT_FACT_REQUIRES_PROJECT_EVIDENCE blocker.
evidence.why: Confirmed test evidence proves evidence scope enforcement prevents unverified claims.

evidence.summary: Previous dispatch artifact shows boundary.project_mutation: 'clean', changes: [], readiness.prd_ready: true, and readiness.blockers: [] after all three agents completed with project-scoped evidence.
evidence.why: Confirmed real execution artifact proves boundary audit passed with no mutations detected.

risks: Tool profile restrictions in provider-adapter.ts are passed as CLI args but not independently verified at runtime; boundary audit is defense-in-depth but not prevention.
Filesystem timing in buildBoundarySnapshot could theoretically miss rapid write-then-delete sequences within execution window, though this is a narrow race condition.

result.notes: Cross-check independently verified all 16 explorer evidence records at cited line numbers. No conflicts found. Implementation, tests, and artifact confirm boundary audit detects mutations via SHA-256 digests, generates BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers, enforces evidence scope declarations, and blocks PRD readiness on violations. Two minor risks noted but do not block claim validity.
